summary: A Go-based monorepo project heavily focused on test coverage optimization, utilizing AI agents to generate supplementary test suites.
concerns: excessive focus on code coverage metrics over functional quality
proliferation of agent-generated test directories (glm-agents) cluttering the repository
uncommitted staged and modified files indicating potential instability
test files named 'coverage_boost' suggest artificial coverage inflation
suggestions: clean up or archive the GOrchestra/glm-agents directories to reduce repository noise
refactor 'coverage_boost' tests into meaningful integration or unit tests
commit or stash the pending changes to maintain a clean working tree
shift focus from coverage percentages to mutation testing or property-based testing
